title: Slow-cooker butter chicken
subtitle: Boneless thighs in a rich, mildly spiced tomato cream sauce
excerpt: Boneless chicken thighs slow-cooked in a spiced tomato sauce with garam masala, cumin, coriander, and ginger, then finished with butter and double cream. Six hours on low produces a sauce with more body than a quick pan version. Mild enough for children.
sourceNotes: Butter chicken (murgh makhani) was created in Delhi in the 1950s. The British adaptation uses the same principle (marinated chicken in a rich tomato and cream sauce) but at a lower heat level than the original. It became one of the most popular restaurant curries in Britain and is now widely cooked at home. The slow cooker produces a sauce that has had time to develop complexity without the risk of reducing too far or burning.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze without the cream. Add the cream fresh when reheating.
recipe.batchNotes: Double the recipe. Freeze without the cream and add fresh when reheating.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cook the curry base the day before. Add cream when reheating.

content.text: Fry the spices in the butter before adding the other ingredients. This blooming step (one minute in hot fat) makes the spices fragrant and distributes their flavour into the sauce rather than leaving them tasting raw. The slow cooker cannot do this step, so it must happen on the hob first.

Melt the {{unsalted-butter}} in a small pan over medium heat. Add the {{onion}} and fry for 4 minutes. Add the {{garlic}}, {{ginger-root}}, {{garam-masala}}, {{cumin-ground}}, {{coriander-ground}}, {{chilli-powder}}, and {{tomato-puree}} and stir for 1 minute. Transfer to the slow cooker. Add the {{chicken-thighs-boneless}}, {{tinned-tomatoes}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Cook on low for 6 hours, until the chicken is tender and pulling apart. Stir the {{double-cream}} through at the end. Taste and adjust seasoning.

items.cause: Tinned tomatoes released a lot of liquid
items.fix: Remove the lid and cook on high for 20 to 30 minutes to reduce. The sauce should coat the back of a spoon

items.cause: Tinned tomatoes were very acidic, or not enough cream
items.fix: Stir in another 50 ml double cream and a pinch of caster sugar. Butter at the finish also softens the acidity

items.cause: Breast meat used instead of thighs, or overcooked
items.fix: Boneless thighs are the correct cut for slow cooker curries. Breast meat overcooks in 6 hours and becomes stringy. Use thighs

content.text: Butter chicken is the most widely eaten restaurant curry in Britain and one of the most cooked at home. The slow cooker version requires less attention than a stovetop version and produces a deeper-flavoured sauce. Serve with basmati rice and naan, and a small bowl of mango chutney and yoghurt alongside.
